item.title: Lithium Coin Batteries Recalled Due to Battery Ingestion; Violates Federal Statute for Child-Resistant Packaging of Coin Batteries; Imported by Proudly American Store, of Canada
item.company: Online at Amazon.com from May 2025 through February 2026 for about $11.
item.brand: Online at Amazon.com from May 2025 through February 2026 for about $11.
item.summary: This recall involves Panasonic-branded lithium coin batteries. The three-volt silver batteries were sold in clear plastic bags with one or two batteries. The brand name and "VL2020" are embossed on the battery. Panasonic is not alleged to have engaged in any wrongdoing.
item.affectedPeople: Consumers who purchased: VL2020 Lithium Coin Batteries.
item.remedy: Consumers should stop using the coin batteries immediately, place them in an area that children cannot access and contact Proudly American Store for a full refund. Consumers will be asked to dispose of the batteries according to local hazardous waste guidelines. Note: Button cell batteries are hazardous. Batteries should be disposed of or recycled by following local hazardous waste procedures.
item.payoutAmount: Repair, refund, replacement, or other remedy if stated by the CPSC notice
